hints.text: Bollard: **red strip wraps around the top** (unique to NZ), thin yellow/white reflector on front or back
Plates white w/ black text (appear mostly white through the blur)
Poles = concrete w/ **one long indent** + small silver **possum guards**; wooden & holey poles also exist
**Give-way signs red text** (Australia = black); **give-way triangles painted on the road** (AU doesn't do this)
Warning signs diamond yellow on **white posts**; **blue street signs** common (esp. rural) — Australia almost never blue
White outer lines; **yellow dashed outer** in towns; inner = single dashed white or **double yellow** (AU has no yellow inner, snow aside)
Chevrons **black-on-yellow with a thin black border** (Australia = same colours, no border)
Green directional signs (state-highway # in a **red crest**); **bridge markers** (yellow = river name); brown signs = landmarks
Two-digit **state-highway numbers run N→S**; **Fonterra farm-sign** first digit = region
Phone = 7 digits + 2-digit area code (02 = mobile); city rubbish bins & bus-stop signs identify Auckland/Wellington/Christchurch
**Southern Alps** snowy range (South Island); West Coast temperate **rainforest**; Waikato/Northland dry rolling hills; **Mt Taranaki** smooth cone; teal glacial lakes near Queenstown
Pole stickers & possum-guard styles pinpoint region (Gisborne yellow cow-tag; Marlborough olive-brown guard; Southland cylindrical); Canterbury = tall hedge rows on flat plains
